task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI application is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the GUI is in a multi-panel layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple distinct widget panels (e.g., Time Series, FFT Plot, etc.) are visible simultaneously within the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opens the widget selection dropdown in one of the panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu listing available widgets is open in the top-left corner of one of the widget panels.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Band Power widget is successfully displayed in one of the panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the widget panels has the title 'Band Power' at the top.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Band Power widget visualizes the classic EEG frequency bands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Band Power widget displays bar graphs or visual indicators labeled with the five frequency bands: Delta, Theta, Alpha, Beta, and Gamma.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and interactions within the OpenBCI GUI window, rather than terminal commands or text editor modifications to config files.

integrity.description: Ensure the Band Power widget is genuinely rendering the UI elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Band Power widget displays actual graphical bars and standard OpenBCI UI fonts, not text pasted over the screen or a mocked-up image.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just write the frequency bands into a text file or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The frequency bands (Delta, Theta, Alpha, Beta, Gamma) appear exclusively within the graphical interface of the OpenBCI Band Power widget.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI GUI (v5.2.2) 'Band Power' widget displays five frequency bands. The actual UI labels rendered by the software are Delta (0.5-4Hz), Theta (4-8Hz), Alpha (8-13Hz), Beta (13-32Hz), and Gamma (32-100Hz). You must accept these UI labels as correct, even though the Alpha, Beta, and Gamma ranges differ slightly from the text in the task description.
